The latest announcement is out from Prosperity Investment Holdings Limited ( (HK:0310) ).

Prosperity Investment Holdings Limited reported that its unaudited consolidated net asset value per share stood at approximately HK$0.029 as at 31 March 2026. The calculation is based on 621,132,020 shares in issue, reflecting the enlarged capital base after shares were issued under a placing completed on 14 October 2025.

The disclosure of net asset value provides investors with an updated snapshot of the company’s underlying asset position on a per-share basis. This information helps shareholders and potential investors assess valuation, track the impact of prior equity placings on net asset backing, and gauge how capital structure changes may influence future returns.

More about Prosperity Investment Holdings Limited

Prosperity Investment Holdings Limited is an investment holding company incorporated in Bermuda and listed on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The company operates as an investment vehicle, deploying capital into various assets with the aim of generating returns for its shareholders within the Hong Kong and broader regional markets.

FWD Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1828) ) has issued an update.

FWD Group Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 29 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025. The agenda also includes re-election of a broad slate of executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, the election of a new non-executive director, and approval for the board to set directors’ and auditor remuneration.

Shareholders will additionally vote on granting the board a general mandate to issue new shares and related securities within prescribed limits under Hong Kong listing rules. The resolutions, if passed, will refresh the company’s capital-raising flexibility and reaffirm its existing governance structure, providing continuity in leadership and potentially supporting future strategic and financing activities.

The latest update is out from Omnivision Integrated Circuits Group, Inc. Class H ( (HK:0501) ).

OmniVision Integrated Circuits Group, Inc., a PRC-based semiconductor company listed in Hong Kong, has convened its 2026 annual general meeting at its Shanghai technology park to review board and independent director work reports and consider the 2025 annual report and profit distribution plan. The agenda also includes approving a three-year shareholder return framework, confirming directors’ pay, reappointing the auditor, and granting guarantees and bank credit lines for controlled subsidiaries.

Shareholders will vote on changing the use and cancellation of repurchased shares, appointing Dr. Gao Wenbao as an executive director and authorized representative, and adopting new remuneration and resignation policies for directors and senior management. A special resolution seeks a broad share issuance mandate of up to 20% of existing share capital, which could provide funding flexibility and support the company’s strategic growth and capital management initiatives while potentially diluting existing holdings if exercised in full.

data.title: Guoquan Food Controlling Shareholder Cuts Stake, Group Agrees to 180-Day Lock-Up
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Guoquan Food (Shanghai) Company., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2517) ) has provided an announcement.

Controlling shareholder Shanghai Guoxiaoquan Enterprise Management Center agreed to sell 124,500,000 Guoquan Food shares, or about 4.74% of the company’s issued share capital, via a block trade at HK$3.80 per share, cutting its stake from 12.61% to 7.88%. Despite the sale, the concert-party group of controlling shareholders will still control around 44.66% of voting rights, and the company expects no material operational impact while highlighting that the transaction should increase free float and improve share liquidity.

Following the disposal, the controlling shareholders, including Mr. Yang, Mr. Meng, Mr. Li, Guoquan Industry, Guoxiaoquan EM and Guoxiaoquan Tech, have voluntarily committed to a 180-day lock-up on any further share disposals from April 16 to October 12, 2026. This lock-up is presented as a signal of their confidence in Guoquan Food’s long-term value and development prospects, though the company has cautioned shareholders and potential investors to exercise care when trading its securities.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2517) stock is a Buy with a HK$4.80 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Guoquan Food (Shanghai) Company., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2517 Stock Forecast page.

More about Guoquan Food (Shanghai) Company., Ltd. Class H

Guoquan Food (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. is a Chinese joint stock company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the food sector. The company focuses on producing and selling consumer food products, serving retail and possibly catering channels in the domestic market, and is controlled by a group of shareholders led by chairperson and CEO Yang Mingchao.

Mokingran Jewellery Group Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:2585) ) has shared an announcement.

Mokingran Jewellery Group has declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.3 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 8 May 2026. The dividend for Hong Kong H-share investors will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 25 June 2026, with the ex-dividend date set for 12 May, a record date of 19 May and a book closure period from 14 to 19 May.

The company has detailed the withholding tax treatment for non-resident enterprise and individual H-shareholders, generally applying a 10% enterprise income tax and individual income tax rates of 10% or 20% depending on the relevant tax treaties with the PRC. This clarification provides greater visibility on net dividend receipts for overseas investors and underscores the company’s intent to return cash to shareholders while complying with cross-border tax requirements.

data.title: Wai Chun Group Partners with Xavvi to Launch AI-Driven Influencer Commerce Platform
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Wai Chun Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1013) ) is now available.

Wai Chun Group Holdings has entered into a strategic cooperation with U.S.-based Xavvi US Inc to establish a holding platform company focused on AI-driven creator economy and influencer traffic monetisation. The partnership will build a global influencer matrix of more than 30,000 personalities, including celebrities, athletes and supermodels, and integrate these resources with Wai Chun’s listed-company platform.

The new platform will operate under a “One Star, One Store” model, creating independent digital brand stores for each influencer and using a Factory-to-Consumer structure to bypass intermediaries and support live-streaming e-commerce and brand endorsement. Revenue will primarily come from a share of gross merchandise value collected centrally by the platform, which targets a high gross profit margin of about 40%, signalling a potential shift in Wai Chun’s earnings profile and its positioning within the fast-growing AI-enabled influencer commerce segment.

More about Wai Chun Group Holdings Limited

Wai Chun Group Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates through various subsidiaries and is currently pursuing business transformation toward internet technology and AI-driven creator-economy ventures. The group is positioning itself to tap into influencer-based e-commerce and traffic monetisation by partnering with emerging technology operators and leveraging digital platforms for new profit drivers.

data.title: Kangqiao Service Seeks PRC Supreme Court Retrial Over RMB473.7 Million Subsidiary Judgment
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kangqiao Service Group Limited ( (HK:2205) ) has provided an announcement.

Kangqiao Service Group Limited has updated investors on ongoing litigation involving its wholly owned mainland subsidiary after Chinese courts previously held the unit liable for RMB473.7 million under loan guarantees linked to Henan Chengqiao. Controlling shareholder and chairman Song Gewei has undertaken to indemnify the listed company for any losses arising from this judgment, aiming to ring‑fence the financial impact at the shareholder level.

The company has applied to the Supreme People’s Court of China for a retrial, arguing that the appellate court misapplied PRC company law, misjudged the relationship between Henan Chengqiao and the subsidiary, and erred in finding the bank to be a good‑faith counterparty. It has also submitted new evidence challenging the validity of the underlying loan agreements, while noting that about RMB212 million of the subsidiary’s bank balances are under judicial enforcement but asserting that the issue remains confined to this unit and does not affect the group’s other operations.

More about Kangqiao Service Group Limited

Kangqiao Service Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed property services provider incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating in mainland China through subsidiaries such as Kangqiao Yueshenghuo Service Group Co., Ltd. The group focuses on community and property management services, with its operations organized across multiple operating entities within the wider Kangqiao Service Group.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Lygend Resources & Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2245) ) just unveiled an update.

Lygend Resources & Technology Co., Ltd., a Chinese nickel-focused resources and technology group, operates across procurement, production and sales, and has built a key international presence through its joint Obi Project in Indonesia. The company positions itself to serve the evolving global nickel and battery materials market by leveraging long-term partnerships and integrated project development.

The board issued a voluntary update stating that Lygend’s production and operations remain normal despite market concerns over a change in Indonesia’s benchmark nickel ore pricing policy. Management emphasized that the company has been actively assessing policy risks, diversifying across key operational areas, and relying on its long-standing, trust-based partnership in the Obi Project to safeguard project continuity and returns, while cautioning investors to remain prudent when trading its shares.

The announcement underlines that Lygend and its Indonesian partners continuously monitor risk factors affecting the Obi Project and adjust arrangements through close consultation. By highlighting operational resilience and strong cross-border collaboration, the company aims to reassure shareholders and prospective investors about its ability to navigate regulatory shifts in a critical supply region for the global nickel market.

More about Lygend Resources & Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Lygend Resources & Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based resources and technology company engaged in the nickel industry, with operations spanning procurement, production and sales along the nickel value chain. The group has a significant international footprint through long-term cooperation with Indonesian partners, notably via joint investment and development of the Obi nickel project, which targets growing demand for nickel in metals and battery-related markets.

The company focuses on maintaining stable operations amid shifting commodity and policy environments by proactively managing macroeconomic and regulatory risks across upstream and downstream segments, and by diversifying its business exposure through integrated resource projects such as Obi in Indonesia.

data.title: COSCO SHIPPING Energy Orders Two Panamax Crude Oil Tankers in Connected Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation Co ( (HK:1138) ) just unveiled an update.

COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation plans to sign shipbuilding contracts with Qidong Offshore, an indirect wholly owned unit of its controlling shareholder COSCO SHIPPING, for the construction of two Panamax crude oil tankers. The vessels, with a combined price of RMB1,018 million, will expand the group’s crude oil fleet and support its energy transportation operations.

Because Qidong Offshore is a connected person under Hong Kong listing rules, the transactions are classified as discloseable and connected, triggering notification, reporting and announcement obligations. The deals are aggregated with previous related transactions for compliance purposes, with applicable percentage ratios above 5% but below 25%, implying moderate scale and regulatory scrutiny without changing the overall transaction classification.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1138) stock is a Buy with a HK$26.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation Co stock, see the HK:1138 Stock Forecast page.

Hi Sun Technology (China) Limited ( (HK:0818) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Hi Sun Technology (China) Limited has called its annual general meeting for 20 May 2026 in Hong Kong to adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and receive the reports of its directors and auditor. Shareholders will also vote on the re-election of four directors, the authorization of director remuneration, and the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor.

In addition to routine governance items, the board is seeking a general mandate to allot, issue and deal with additional shares, including the sale or transfer of treasury shares, up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding any treasury shares. If approved, this mandate would give the board added flexibility to raise capital or pursue corporate actions, potentially affecting future dilution levels and signaling a readiness to act on strategic or funding opportunities within the fintech sector.

More about Hi Sun Technology (China) Limited

Hi Sun Technology (China) Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the technology sector, providing electronic payment and related financial technology solutions to the mainland China and regional markets. The company serves banks, merchants and other institutional clients, positioning itself within the broader fintech and payment services industry.

The latest announcement is out from Mokingran Jewellery Group Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:2585) ).

Mokingran Jewellery Group has called its 2025 annual general meeting for May 8, 2026 in Shandong Province, where shareholders will review the board’s work report, the 2025 annual report and a proposed profit distribution plan. The agenda also covers approval of 2026 composite credit facilities, a guarantee plan, an annual cap for futures and derivatives trading, and the reappointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor.

Shareholders will additionally vote on a special resolution granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s existing share capital, including through convertible securities, within a defined period and subject to regulatory approvals. The mandate, if passed, would give Mokingran added flexibility for potential financing and capital management initiatives, which could influence its funding structure and future growth options.

More about Mokingran Jewellery Group Co Ltd Class H

Mokingran Jewellery Group Co., Ltd. is a China-based jewellery company listed in Hong Kong, focusing on the design, manufacture and sale of gold and jewellery products. The group serves the domestic consumer market from its base in Shandong Province and operates within the broader precious metals and retail jewellery industry.
